purpose_context: Spec Kitty's MVP launch blocker (planning#17) showed that CLI emitted user-driven review rejections (in_review→planned) without force while SaaS rejected them as unforced backward transitions and dumped them into terminal_failed. The root cause is that no single source of truth defines what WPStatusChanged means for backward transitions, the role of actor=user, when force is required, and how reconciliation diagnostics are recorded. This mission defines that contract and provides conformance fixtures so every consumer implements the same semantics.
purpose_tldr: Lock the canonical WPStatusChanged event contract for backward transitions, force, actor, and reconciliation so CLI and SaaS share one interpretation.
